# RFC — [CHANGE TITLE]

> Request for Comments. Filed before implementation of significant changes.
> Circulate for feedback before writing code. Approved RFC = green light to build.

**Author:** [name]  
**Date:** [YYYY-MM-DD]  
**Status:** [Draft | Comment Period | Accepted | Rejected | Withdrawn]  
**Comment Period Closes:** [YYYY-MM-DD]  
**Phase:** [P0–P7]

---

## Motivation

[Why is this change needed now? What problem does it solve, and why is the current
approach insufficient? Link to issues, incidents, or user feedback that motivates this.]

---

## Proposed Change

[Concrete description of what you want to change. Be specific enough that a reviewer can
identify exactly what will be different after this RFC is implemented.]

**Scope:**
- In scope: [what this RFC covers]
- Out of scope: [what it explicitly does not cover]

---

## Detailed Design

[Technical depth. How will this work? Include code sketches, schema changes, API changes,
or algorithm descriptions as needed. This section should give reviewers enough to say
"yes, I understand how this would be implemented."]

```python
# Example interface or pseudocode
```

---

## Drawbacks

[Honest assessment of what this change makes harder or worse. Every significant change
has drawbacks. A RFC that claims no drawbacks is not credible.]

---

## Alternatives

**Why not [alternative approach]?**
[Reason — be specific. "It doesn't fit our Architectural Constitution" is a valid reason
if you can point to which value it violates.]

**Why now?**
[Why is this the right time for this change? What makes this urgent vs. deferrable?]

---

## Unresolved Questions

[Questions that need to be answered before this RFC can be accepted. These are blockers.
Flag questions that can be resolved during implementation separately.]

- [ ] [Blocking question 1]
- [ ] [Implementation-time question]

---

## Implementation Plan (Draft)

[High-level sequencing — not detailed task breakdown. That belongs in the design doc or sprint plan.]

1. [Phase 1]
2. [Phase 2]
3. [Migration / cutover]

---

## References

- [Prior RFC or design doc this builds on]
- [External standard or prior art]
- [Relevant ADR]

---

*Comment here or in the associated PR. Comments close [date].*
*After comment period: author updates RFC to reflect decisions, then files ADR.*
